title: Smoky Velvety Portuguese Tomato Soup
description: A onepot, Jainfriendly Portuguese tomato soup that is smoky, velvety, and absolutely comforting.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced bell pepper and sauté for about 2 minutes, just until slightly softened.
Pour in the passata tomato puree, stir, and let it simmer for 5 minutes to caramelize the tomato flavors.
Add the vegetable broth, coconut milk, smoked paprika, cumin, bay leaf, and sea salt; stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes to meld flavors.
While the soup simmers, blanch the bell pepper: boil water, add the pepper for 2 minutes, transfer to ice water, peel off the skin and discard it.
Remove the bay leaf and stir the blanched bell pepper back into the pot.
Using an immersion blender, puree the soup until silky smooth; if you prefer, transfer in batches to a countertop blender.
Season with black pepper and adjust salt if needed; stir in an additional splash of coconut milk for extra velvety texture.
Ladle the soup into bowls, garnish with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired, and serve hot.
